key_points: A triangle is a polygon with three sides and three angles
Sum of interior angles of a triangle = 180°; sum of exterior angles = 360°
Types based on sides: equilateral, isosceles, scalene
Types based on angles: acute, right, obtuse
Triangles are congruent if they satisfy SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, RHS
Triangles are similar if corresponding angles are equal and sides are proportional
Median, altitude, bisector, and perpendicular bisector are important line segments in triangles
examples: Equilateral triangle: all sides = a, all angles = 60°
Isosceles triangle: two sides = 5 cm, base = 6 cm; angles opposite equal sides are equal
Scalene triangle: sides 4 cm, 5 cm, 6 cm; all sides and angles unequal
Similar triangles: ΔABC ~ ΔPQR if ∠A=∠P, ∠B=∠Q, AB/PQ=BC/QR=AC/PR
exam_tips: Use congruence and similarity conditions carefully to solve problems
Label all sides and angles in diagrams
Always check triangle inequality before constructing a triangle
For Heron’s formula, calculate semi-perimeter first
Use median, altitude, and bisector formulas when needed in advanced problems
